neighborhoods.name: El Centro / La Alpujarra
neighborhoods.description: Downtown core with government buildings, Botero Plaza, and the bustling commercial district
neighborhoods.character: Dense urban, commercial, transit hub with Metro access
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Medellín. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Envigado
neighborhoods.description: Southern municipality with small-town charm, excellent walkability, and a thriving local food scene
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Medellín's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Belen
neighborhoods.description: Large residential zone west of El Poblado with parks, local markets, and family-friendly atmosphere
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: El Poblado
neighborhoods.description: Upscale hillside neighborhood that is the primary expat hub, with international restaurants and modern amenities

housing.predominantType: Modern high-rise condos, gated communities, luxury apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
